title: Returning a Faulty Kettle
summary: A customer returns a broken electric kettle to a shop and asks for a refund.

body.speaker: Sophie
body.text: Excuse me, I bought this kettle here last week. It stopped working after two days.

body.speaker: Marcus
body.text: Oh, I'm sorry to hear that. Do you have your receipt?

body.speaker: Sophie
body.text: Yes, here it is. I paid twenty-five dollars for it.

body.speaker: Marcus
body.text: Thank you. What exactly is wrong with the kettle?

body.speaker: Sophie
body.text: The power button doesn't work anymore. It won't turn on at all.

body.speaker: Marcus
body.text: I see. That sounds like a manufacturing defect. Would you like a refund or an exchange?

body.speaker: Sophie
body.text: I'd prefer a full refund, please. I don't trust this brand anymore.

body.speaker: Marcus
body.text: That's completely understandable. I'll process your refund now. Can you show me a valid ID, please?

body.speaker: Sophie
body.text: Of course. Here is my driver's license.

body.speaker: Marcus
body.text: Perfect. The refund will go back to your card in three to five business days.

body.speaker: Sophie
body.text: Can I get a cash refund instead?

body.speaker: Marcus
body.text: I'm afraid we can only refund to the original payment method.

body.speaker: Sophie
body.text: Okay, that's fine. Thank you for your help.

body.speaker: Marcus
body.text: You're welcome. Sorry again for the trouble with the kettle.

sentences.speaker: Sophie
sentences.text: Excuse me, I bought this kettle here last week.
sentences.translationVi: Xin lỗi, tôi đã mua ấm đun nước này ở đây tuần trước.

sentences.speaker: Sophie
sentences.text: It stopped working after two days.
sentences.translationVi: Nó đã ngừng hoạt động sau hai ngày.

sentences.speaker: Marcus
sentences.text: Oh, I'm sorry to hear that. Do you have your receipt?
sentences.translationVi: Ồ, tôi rất tiếc khi nghe điều đó. Bạn có hóa đơn không?

sentences.speaker: Sophie
sentences.text: Yes, here it is. I paid twenty-five dollars for it.
sentences.translationVi: Vâng, đây rồi. Tôi đã trả hai mươi lăm đô la cho nó.

sentences.speaker: Marcus
sentences.text: Thank you. What exactly is wrong with the kettle?
sentences.translationVi: Cảm ơn bạn. Chính xác thì ấm đun có vấn đề gì?

sentences.speaker: Sophie
sentences.text: The power button doesn't work anymore. It won't turn on at all.
sentences.translationVi: Nút nguồn không còn hoạt động nữa. Nó hoàn toàn không bật lên được.

sentences.speaker: Marcus
sentences.text: I see. That sounds like a manufacturing defect.
sentences.translationVi: Tôi hiểu. Điều đó có vẻ như là lỗi sản xuất.

sentences.speaker: Marcus
sentences.text: Would you like a refund or an exchange?
sentences.translationVi: Bạn muốn hoàn tiền hay đổi hàng?

sentences.speaker: Sophie
sentences.text: I'd prefer a full refund, please.
sentences.translationVi: Tôi muốn hoàn tiền đầy đủ, làm ơn.

sentences.speaker: Sophie
sentences.text: I don't trust this brand anymore.
sentences.translationVi: Tôi không còn tin tưởng thương hiệu này nữa.

sentences.speaker: Marcus
sentences.text: That's completely understandable. I'll process your refund now.
sentences.translationVi: Điều đó hoàn toàn có thể hiểu được. Tôi sẽ xử lý hoàn tiền cho bạn ngay bây giờ.

sentences.speaker: Marcus
sentences.text: Can you show me a valid ID, please?
sentences.translationVi: Bạn có thể cho tôi xem giấy tờ tùy thân hợp lệ không?

sentences.speaker: Sophie
sentences.text: Of course. Here is my driver's license.
sentences.translationVi: Tất nhiên rồi. Đây là bằng lái xe của tôi.

sentences.speaker: Marcus
sentences.text: Perfect. The refund will go back to your card in three to five business days.
sentences.translationVi: Hoàn hảo. Khoản hoàn tiền sẽ được trả lại vào thẻ của bạn trong vòng ba đến năm ngày làm việc.

sentences.speaker: Sophie
sentences.text: Can I get a cash refund instead?
sentences.translationVi: Tôi có thể nhận hoàn tiền bằng tiền mặt thay thế không?

sentences.speaker: Marcus
sentences.text: I'm afraid we can only refund to the original payment method.
sentences.translationVi: Tôi e rằng chúng tôi chỉ có thể hoàn tiền theo phương thức thanh toán ban đầu.

sentences.speaker: Sophie
sentences.text: Okay, that's fine. Thank you for your help.
sentences.translationVi: Được rồi, không sao. Cảm ơn bạn đã giúp đỡ.

sentences.speaker: Marcus
sentences.text: You're welcome. Sorry again for the trouble with the kettle.
sentences.translationVi: Không có gì. Xin lỗi lần nữa vì sự cố với ấm đun nước.

hintVocab.meaningVi: phương thức thanh toán ban đầu

grammarNotes.title: Simple Past for Recent Events
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng thì quá khứ đơn (V-ed hoặc dạng bất quy tắc) để nói về sự kiện đã xảy ra trong quá khứ. Ví dụ: 'I bought this kettle last week' (mua – bought), 'It stopped working' (dừng – stopped).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the simple past tense for completed actions in the past. 'I bought this kettle last week' uses the irregular past of 'buy'. 'It stopped working' uses the regular past of 'stop'.

grammarNotes.title: would like vs. want
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Would you like…?' lịch sự hơn 'Do you want…?' khi hỏi về sở thích hoặc đưa ra lựa chọn trong bối cảnh dịch vụ khách hàng.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Would you like a refund or an exchange?' is more polite than 'Do you want…?' Use 'would like' in customer service situations to sound professional and courteous.

grammarNotes.title: I'd prefer + noun
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'I'd prefer + danh từ' để diễn đạt sở thích cụ thể. 'I'd prefer a full refund' = 'I would prefer a full refund'. Lịch sự và trực tiếp hơn 'I want'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I'd prefer' is a contraction of 'I would prefer'. It expresses a polite preference. Follow it with a noun: 'I'd prefer a full refund', or an infinitive: 'I'd prefer to get a refund'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Do you think shops should always offer a full refund for faulty products, or should there be a time limit? Why?
